title: Parallel read-only tool execution (ToolDef.readOnly + executeToolBatch + per-role flag)
description: Replace the loop's strictly serial tool execution with a delegating executor that runs contiguous runs of read-only-annotated tool calls concurrently while everything else stays serial. Classification is a ToolDef.readOnly annotation (ADR-2, conservative allow-list: read_file/glob/grep only — bash never). Results preserve original order, per-tool failures become in-slot error results, and the batch never rejects. Enabled by a per-role parallelReadOnlyTools config flag, default false → byte-identical serial semantics.

successCriteria.description: ToolDef supports optional readOnly?: boolean; exactly read_file, glob and grep are annotated readOnly:true in the tool schemas; bash, write_file and edit_file are unmarked.

With parallel enabled, a turn requesting multiple read-only tools executes them concurrently — proven by delayed fake handlers where total elapsed is less than the sum of individual delays — and the returned ToolResult[] preserves the original tool-call order by toolUseId.

A throwing handler or unknown tool inside a parallel batch produces an isError ToolResult in its original slot with the same shape as the serial path's error results, and executeToolBatch resolves (never rejects).
With the flag off, or for any unmarked tool, execution order and results are byte-identical to the pre-change serial for-await loop (all existing loop/tool tests pass unchanged).
A per-role parallelReadOnlyTools boolean config field (optional, default false) reaches AgenticLoopParams; existing configs parse unchanged.
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass.

nonGoals: Do not annotate bash as read-only under any circumstance (a shell command can mutate state).
Do not parallelize write tools or mixed batches beyond contiguous read-only runs.
Do not hard-code a tool-name allow-list inside the loop (ADR-2: classification travels with the ToolDef).
Do not enable the flag by default anywhere.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: annotation coverage, proven concurrency with order preservation, in-slot error containment without batch rejection, byte-identical serial fallback, and the config flag path.
definitionOfDone: Tool execution is delegated to executeToolBatch; with the per-role flag on, independent read-only calls overlap in time while results keep their original order and failures stay in-slot; with the flag off or annotations absent, behavior is byte-identical to the serial loop. Build and full suite green.
assumptions: The serial loop lives at src/orchestrator/agentic-loop.ts ~:353-388 with the unknown-tool error path (~:362-369) and per-tool catch (~:379-387) whose result shapes the executor must mirror exactly.
Tool schemas live in src/orchestrator/tools/schemas.ts (~:9-162) and handlers in src/orchestrator/tools/handlers.ts (ToolHandler ~:21-23).
graph_* tools (src/graph/prompts.ts context) are NOT annotated this sprint — they can opt in later per ADR-2's self-declaration model.
onToolUse callback timing may shift for parallel batches (fired at dispatch); existing tests observe presence/args, not strict wall-clock interleaving.
outOfScope: Parallelizing MCP-bridged tools (sprint 10 tools default to serial via absent annotation).
Cross-turn pipelining or speculative tool execution.
generatorNotes: Create src/orchestrator/tools/executor.ts exporting executeToolBatch(batch: { toolCalls, toolHandlers, readOnlyTools: Set<string>, parallel: boolean, onToolUse? }): Promise<ToolResult[]>. Algorithm: walk toolCalls grouping maximal contiguous runs where parallel && readOnlyTools.has(name); execute such runs with Promise.all over wrapped handlers (each wrapper try/catches into the exact isError ToolResult shape used by the serial catch), everything else strictly sequential; assemble results indexed by position so order is preserved. Add readOnly?: boolean to the ToolDef type (schemas.ts) and annotate read_file/glob/grep only. In agentic-loop.ts, derive readOnlyTools once from params.tools and replace the ~:353-388 block with a call to executeToolBatch (parallel = params.parallelReadOnlyTools === true); keep the ToolResultMessage append (~:392) unchanged. Add the per-role Zod flag next to sprint 3's effort/budget fields (coordinate: if sprint 3 already landed, extend the same section; if building independently, add just this flag additively) and thread it from the generator entry point. Use fake handlers with setTimeout delays in tests; measure elapsed with performance.now().
evaluatorNotes: sc-4-1: assert schemas' readOnly flags exactly (three true, bash/write_file/edit_file undefined). sc-4-2: three fake read-only handlers each delayed ~50ms, parallel=true → elapsed < 120ms (vs ~150ms serial) AND results array matches input order with correct toolUseIds; also verify a serial run of the same batch takes >= 140ms to prove the fixture is meaningful. sc-4-3: middle handler throws / middle tool unknown → its slot isError with message shape matching the serial path (compare against a serial-path fixture), other slots normal, promise resolves. sc-4-4: parallel=false and unmarked-tool batches → assert handler invocation order equals input order (record call order); run FULL existing suite. sc-4-5: config fixture without the flag parses unchanged; with flag, spy on runAgenticLoop params. Build + typecheck.
